lede: A look at January 9 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Simone de Beauvoir and Richard Nixon, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Mozambique funeral beer poisoning
events.desc: A mass poisoning at a funeral in Mozambique involving beer that was contaminated with Burkholderia gladioli leaves 75 dead and over 230 people ill.

events.title: Iran Air Flight 277
events.desc: Iran Air Flight 277 crashes near Urmia in the northwest of the country, in icy conditions, killing 78 people.

events.title: Apple Inc.
events.desc: Apple CEO Steve Jobs introduces the original iPhone at a Macworld keynote in San Francisco.

events.title: First Chechen War
events.desc: First Chechen War: Chechen separatists launch a raid against the helicopter airfield and later a civilian hospital in the city of Kizlyar in the neighboring Dagestan, which turns into a massive hostage crisis involving thousands of civilians.

events.title: Iraq
events.desc: Representatives from the United States and Iraq meet at the Geneva Peace Conference to try to find a peaceful resolution to the Iraqi invasion of Kuwait.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: First flight of the Avro Lancaster.

events.title: World War I
events.desc: World War I: The Battle of Gallipoli concludes with an Ottoman Empire victory when the last Allied forces are evacuated from the peninsula.

events.title: 1857 Fort Tejon earthquake
events.desc: The 7.9 Mw Fort Tejon earthquake shakes Central and Southern California with a maximum Mercalli intensity of IX (Violent).

events.title: Connecticut
events.desc: Connecticut becomes the fifth state to ratify the United States Constitution.

events.title: Basel
events.desc: The Jewish population of Basel, believed by the residents to be the cause of the ongoing Black Death, is rounded up and incinerated.

births.name: Richard Nixon
births.line: American commander, lawyer, and politician, 37th President of the United States

births.name: Catherine, Princess of Wales
births.line: Member of the British royal family

deaths.text: Józef Oleksy, Polish economist and politician, 7th Prime Minister of Poland
Johann Strauss III, Austrian violinist, composer, and conductor
Houston Stewart Chamberlain, English-German philosopher and author
Napoleon III, French politician, 1st President of France
Emperor Xizong of Jin, Emperor of Jin from 1135 to 1150

observances.text: Christian feast day: Blessed Alix Le Clerc
Christian feast day: Blessed Giulia della Rena
Christian feast day: Feast of the Black Nazarene (Manila, Philippines)
Christian feast day: Julia Chester Emery (Episcopal Church (USA))
Christian feast day: Stephen (old calendar Eastern Orthodox)
